text: Awesome! :grinning: Can you give a short description of your project that your going to demo/present ?

elements.text: Can you give a short description of your project that your going to demo/present ?

text:
I’d like to share a couple tools that help make it easier to jump between UI and code while building two different products. The approach should apply to any languages, but my tools are written in TypeScript.

So, something like “UI dev tools I don’t see enough in practice”

text: Impressive! What does the code for that look like? I'm curious how you handle tabular stuff...

elements.text: Impressive! What does the code for that look like? I'm curious how you handle tabular stuff...

text:
It’s that layout algorithm where you ask all of your subviews, “How big would you want to be if you had [this much space]?” then tell them, “Okay, I’m giving you [this much space], lay out your subviews.”

I don’t know the name for it! But it meant the table was easy: it just takes max() of all the heights and widths for the cells in every row and column.

I draw the wires in a second pass using the world coordinates of the THREE.Object3Ds post-layout.

text: Oh I guess I was curious mainly about the client code, like you showed for the previous screenshot. Cuz there's no longer a simple tree hierarchy. Those details are cool tho. :+1:
